facts.text: Greene was named after Revolutionary War hero General Nathanael Greene, a change that replaced its original name Hornby in the late 18th century.
The village of Greene was incorporated in 1842, making it one of the oldest incorporated communities in Chenango County.
The Lyon Iron Works, founded in 1840, once produced cannonballs for the Civil War and later became part of a major forklift company.
Page Seed Company, established in 1896, still packages seeds nationwide, a legacy that began in Greene’s agricultural past.
The Sherwood Hotel, recently reopened, is listed on the National Register of Historic Places and is rumored to be haunted by a wartime widow.
In 2006, the Chenango River Theatre opened in Greene, aiming to bring professional, non‑profit theater to a rural setting.
